text: What type of incident occurred with your vehicle?
When exactly did the incident occur? Please provide date and time.
Where did the incident occur? Please share the city and location.
What is the road type at the incident location — highway, urban road, parking lot, or private property?
Can you share a nearby landmark or the exact street name for the incident location?
What is your vehicle's registration number?
What is the make of your vehicle (e.g., Maruti, Hyundai, Tata)?
What is the model of your vehicle (e.g., Swift, Creta, Nexon)?
What year was your vehicle manufactured?
What is the color of your vehicle?
What fuel type does your vehicle use — petrol, diesel, CNG, or electric?
What was the approximate odometer reading at the time of the incident?
Is the vehicle used for personal use, commercial purposes, or ridesharing?
Was the vehicle being driven at the time of the incident, or was it parked?
What were the weather conditions at the time of the incident?
What was the visibility like — clear, foggy, heavy rain, or night-time dark?
Was it daytime or nighttime when the incident occurred?
How many vehicles were involved in the incident in total?
Were there any passengers in your vehicle at the time?
How many passengers were in your vehicle?
Were any of your passengers injured?
Were you injured in the incident?
Did anyone require immediate medical attention at the scene?
Was an ambulance called to the scene?
At what speed was your vehicle approximately travelling at the time of the incident?
Was the vehicle's engine running at the time of the incident?
How far is the incident location from where the vehicle is normally parked or garaged?
Was the vehicle on a public road or inside private premises when the incident occurred?
Did you immediately call the insurance helpline after the incident?
How long after the incident are you reporting this claim?
Has the vehicle been moved from the incident location?
Is the vehicle currently at the incident location, a garage, or your home?
Was the incident a single isolated event or part of a chain of events?
Was the vehicle on an authorized route at the time of the incident?
Was the vehicle loaded with goods or passengers beyond its rated capacity?
Was this vehicle recently purchased? If so, when?
Was the vehicle modified in any way before the incident?
If modified, were these modifications declared to the insurer?
Was the vehicle fitted with any anti-theft or tracking device?
Does the vehicle have a valid Pollution Under Control (PUC) certificate?
Was the vehicle under any finance or loan at the time of the incident?
Can you describe in your own words what happened?
Was the vehicle parked legally at the time of the incident?
Was the vehicle's headlights on at the time of the incident?
Were the vehicle's windscreen wipers functioning properly?
Was the road wet, dry, or waterlogged at the time of the incident?
Were traffic signals, signs, or road markings clearly visible at the location?
Was the incident on a one-way or two-way road?
Were there any road construction or diversions near the incident site?
Was the vehicle towed away from the scene?
Who arranged the towing — you, the insurer, or a third party?
Do you have the towing receipt?
Was the vehicle involved in any incident in the past 6 months?
Has the vehicle had any major repairs or accident history prior to this incident?
Was there any pre-existing damage to the vehicle before this incident?
If pre-existing damage exists, can you describe what was damaged and when it occurred?
Was the vehicle serviced or inspected recently before the incident?
Were the brakes functioning properly before the incident?
Were the tyres in good condition before the incident?
What state/region did the incident occur in?
Was the incident on an expressway or toll road?
Was the vehicle carrying any hazardous materials at the time?
Was the vehicle being driven in reverse when the incident occurred?
Was the vehicle stationary or in motion at the exact moment of impact?
Did the incident happen at an intersection?
Was the vehicle performing a turn when the incident occurred?
Was the incident during peak traffic hours?
Was the vehicle attempting to overtake when the incident occurred?
Did the incident occur in a tunnel, underpass, or bridge?
Was the vehicle in a legal parking zone when damage occurred?
Did the vehicle stall or break down before the incident?
Were any hazard lights or warning signals active before the incident?
Was the vehicle on a slope or incline when the incident occurred?
Was the vehicle in the correct lane at the time of the incident?
Was the incident caused by a sudden mechanical failure?
Was the incident caused by a tyre burst?
Was the vehicle being driven for the first time on this route?
Was the vehicle involved in a previous accident claim this year?
Is the vehicle registered in your name?
If not registered in your name, what is your relationship to the owner?
Was the vehicle on a test drive at the time of the incident?
Did the incident happen during a natural disaster event (cyclone, earthquake)?
Was there a traffic jam or congested conditions at the time of the incident?
Was the vehicle's horn functioning properly?
Were any animals involved in causing the incident?
Did a fallen tree, debris, or obstacle on the road cause the incident?
Was the vehicle in a car wash or service bay when the incident occurred?
Was the vehicle being used for a ridesharing trip (Ola/Uber) at the time?
Did any government or municipal vehicle cause the incident?
Was the incident captured on any nearby CCTV camera?
Was the incident reported to the police at the scene?
Is the vehicle currently drivable?
Was the incident in a residential area, commercial zone, or industrial area?
Were any traffic signs violated before the incident?
Was the vehicle being used at the time of the incident for its insured purpose?
Was the incident self-caused or caused by an external factor?
Was the vehicle involved in the incident while being driven by a learner?
Was the vehicle insured under a fleet policy covering multiple vehicles?
Was the vehicle on a racing track or closed circuit when the incident occurred?
Was the incident within the geographical limits specified in the policy?
From which direction did the primary impact occur — front, rear, left side, right side, or rollover?
What was the approximate speed of your vehicle at the time of impact?
What was the approximate speed of the other vehicle at the time of impact?
Was your vehicle rear-ended by another vehicle?
Was this a head-on collision?
Was this a side-impact or T-bone collision?
Did your vehicle roll over after the impact?
Was your vehicle hit from behind while stationary?
Did the collision involve more than two vehicles?
Was the collision caused by a vehicle merging into your lane?
Was the traffic signal red, green, or amber when the incident occurred at the intersection?
Did you apply brakes before the collision?
Did the vehicle skid before or during the collision?
Was the collision caused by the other vehicle jumping a red light?
Was there a chain collision — did your vehicle hit a third vehicle after the initial impact?
Did the collision occur while changing lanes?
Was your vehicle attempting to park when the collision occurred?
Was the collision caused by a vehicle pulling out from a side street?
Was the collision caused by a vehicle overtaking unsafely?
Was the collision a result of wrong-way driving by the other vehicle?
Was the impact a glancing blow or a full impact?
Did the vehicle mount a kerb or divider during or after the collision?
Was the collision caused by sudden braking of the vehicle ahead?
Was your vehicle rear-ending another vehicle, or was your vehicle rear-ended?
Was the collision at a zebra crossing or pedestrian crossing?
Did the airbags in your vehicle deploy during the collision?
Was the seat belt worn by the driver and passengers at the time of the collision?
Did the vehicle come to rest against a wall, median, or another vehicle?
Was the collision due to a pothole or damaged road surface?
Was there any debris on the road that contributed to the collision?
Was the other vehicle a two-wheeler, four-wheeler, truck, or bus?
What was the angle of impact — approximately 90 degrees, 45 degrees, or direct?
Did the collision cause your vehicle to spin?
Was the collision in a school zone or hospital zone?
Was the collision caused by a tyre blowout on the other vehicle?
Did the collision happen in a no-overtaking zone?
Was the other vehicle parked or stationary at the time of the collision?
Did your vehicle hit a stationary object (wall, pole, divider)?
What type of stationary object did the vehicle hit?
Was the collision on a flyover or elevated road?
Was the collision inside a multi-level parking structure?
Did the other vehicle flee the scene immediately after the collision?
Did you try to avoid the collision with evasive action?
Describe the evasive action taken — swerved left, swerved right, braked hard?
Was your vehicle pushed into another lane or vehicle due to the impact?
Was the collision on a divided highway with a median?
Was the collision a result of a vehicle not giving way at a roundabout?
Was the collision caused by blinding headlights from an oncoming vehicle?
Did the vehicle come to a complete stop before the collision, or was it still moving?
How many points of impact did the vehicle sustain?
Was the collision inside a compound, gated community, or campus?
Was the collision caused by an animal suddenly crossing the road?
Was the collision caused by a pedestrian stepping onto the road suddenly?
Was the collision in the same direction of traffic (rear-end) or opposite direction (head-on)?
Was the other vehicle a heavy commercial vehicle (HCV) or light commercial vehicle (LCV)?
Did the collision cause a fuel leak from your vehicle?
Was the collision caused by door-opening into traffic (door-swiping)?
Was the vehicle on the correct side of the road at the time of collision?
Did any vehicle involved cross a solid centre line?
Was the collision in a bus lane or cycle lane?
Was the collision impact absorbed by the bumper, or did it penetrate into the body?
Were there other witnesses to the direction of fault?
Did the police at scene assess the fault direction?
Was the collision caused by a vehicle reversing in the wrong direction?
Did the other driver admit fault at the scene?
Was any exchange of documents done at the scene with the other driver?
Did the collision occur during a U-turn manoeuvre?
Was the collision on a slip road or access ramp to a highway?
Was the collision caused by a vehicle cutting across to exit the road?
Was the vehicle in the leftmost or rightmost lane when the collision occurred?
Were hazard lights on any vehicle prior to the collision?
Was the collision caused by a vehicle running off the road into yours?
Did the collision occur at a speed-breaker or rumble strip?
Was the other vehicle's registration plate visible and noted?
Was the collision in a designated no-parking zone?
Did the collision happen while your vehicle was being driven uphill or downhill?
Was the collision caused by a mechanical defect (brake failure, steering lock)?
Was the vehicle on a bridge or flyover when the incident occurred?
Was the impact accompanied by a loud noise (bang, scraping, crunch)?
Did the vehicle flip onto its side?
Was the collision in a school or hospital drop-off/pickup zone?
Did the vehicle collide with a two-wheeler rider who fell?
Was the collision caused by sudden flooding on the road?
Was the collision during a sudden sandstorm or dust storm?
Was the collision a result of road rage by another driver?
Was the vehicle's dashboard camera recording at the time?
What was the estimated damage cost as assessed at the scene?
Was the collision during an emergency situation (rushing to hospital)?
Was the vehicle driven onto the footpath or pavement during the collision?
Was the collision due to road signage being absent or misleading?
Was the collision in a high-accident or black-spot zone?
Did the collision result in airbag failure (should have deployed but did not)?
Was the vehicle stolen and then involved in a collision by the thief?
Was the vehicle in a collision avoidance system-equipped state?
Was the collision at a toll plaza or entry/exit barrier?
Was the collision inside a tunnel with limited visibility?
Was the collision caused by debris falling from an overhead vehicle?
Was the collision at a railway level crossing?
Was the collision caused by failure of traffic management at a roadwork zone?
Was the collision inside a vehicle ferry or transport vessel?
Which parts of your vehicle were visibly damaged? (Select all that apply: front bumper, rear bumper, doors, hood, etc.)
How would you describe the overall severity of the damage — minor scratch, moderate dent, severe structural, or total loss?
Is the vehicle drivable in its current condition?
Were the airbags deployed during the incident?
Was the windshield cracked or shattered?
Was any other glass (side windows, rear glass) damaged?
Is there any visible damage to the underbody of the vehicle?
Was the front bumper damaged? If so, describe the extent.
Was the rear bumper damaged?
Was the hood (bonnet) dented or damaged?
Was the trunk (boot) damaged or deformed?
Were any doors damaged — which ones (front left, front right, rear left, rear right)?
Was the roof of the vehicle damaged?
Were the headlamps or tail lamps damaged?
Were the alloy wheels or tyres damaged?
Was the engine visibly damaged or leaking fluid?
Did the vehicle come to rest at an angle, suggesting chassis/frame damage?
Was the vehicle's cooling system (radiator) damaged?
Is there visible fuel or oil leakage under the vehicle?
Were the front fenders (wings) damaged?
Was the steering wheel, dashboard, or instrument panel damaged?
Were the seats damaged or displaced?
Were the tyres punctured or blown out in the incident?
Was the exhaust pipe or exhaust system damaged?
Was the fuel tank damaged or punctured?
Was the gearbox or transmission damaged?
Was the suspension system damaged (shock absorbers, struts, springs)?
Were the axles damaged or bent?
Was the brake system damaged?
Was the electrical system (battery, wiring) damaged?
Was the infotainment system or dashboard electronics damaged?
Was there smoke coming from the engine after the incident?
Were any fluids (coolant, engine oil, brake fluid) visibly spilling?
Did the damage require immediate towing, or was the vehicle driven to safety?
What is your estimate of the repair cost?
Have you obtained a formal repair estimate from a workshop?
Which workshop provided the estimate, if any?
Was the damaged area repainted or repaired previously — indicating prior damage?
Was the interior of the vehicle flooded with water?
Was the engine submerged in water at any point?
Were the electronic systems showing warning lights after the incident?
Was the air conditioning system damaged?
Were the wiper blades or wiper motor damaged?
Was the side mirror (ORVM) damaged on either side?
Was the number plate holder or plate itself damaged?
Was the radiator grille damaged?
Was the vehicle's catalytic converter damaged?
Are any panel gaps uneven, suggesting structural frame damage?
Was the door hinge or door frame damaged, preventing doors from opening or closing?
Was the bonnet latch or bonnet strut damaged?
Did the vehicle's warning horn stop working after the incident?
Was the sunroof or moonroof damaged?
Were the fog lamps damaged?
Was the spare tyre damaged or inaccessible due to the incident?
Was the interior carpet or lining damaged due to water or debris?
Was the vehicle's automatic parking sensor or rear camera damaged?
Was the child safety lock or seat belt mechanism damaged?
Was the vehicle's tracking device dislodged or damaged?
Was the vehicle's handbrake cable or mechanism damaged?
Were multiple damage zones affected simultaneously (indicating high-speed impact)?
Is the damage limited to one side, or is it distributed across multiple sides?
Was the vehicle classified as a total loss by the workshop or surveyor?
Was the total loss assessment conducted by a certified surveyor?
Was the damage consistent with the described incident type and direction?
Has the vehicle been repaired already before the surveyor inspection?
Were photos of the damage taken at the scene?
Were videos of the damage recorded?
Was the damage to the vehicle's chassis frame visible (bent A-pillar, B-pillar)?
Is the driver's side or passenger's side more heavily damaged?
Was the windshield wiper control arm broken?
Was the licence plate illumination light damaged?
Were the tyres on one specific axle (front or rear) more damaged?
Was the vehicle's central locking system damaged?
Were the window regulators (automatic window mechanisms) damaged?
Was the damage caused to the vehicle during rescue operations (cutting open door)?
Is the vehicle leaning to one side due to spring or strut damage?
Was the speedometer or odometer cluster damaged?
Was the vehicle's reverse gear system damaged?
Was the vehicle's power steering system damaged?
Was the battery under the bonnet displaced or cracked?
Was the alternator or generator damaged?
Was the AC compressor visibly cracked or detached?
Was the gear shift lever or gear selector damaged?
Was the damage to the exterior paintwork only, or does it involve denting?
Was the vehicle's fuel cap or fuel filler neck damaged?
Are the door seals damaged, causing water ingress risk?
Was the vehicle's drivetrain (driveshaft, CV joints) damaged?
Was the vehicle's anti-lock braking system (ABS) sensor damaged?
Was the vehicle's electronic stability control system showing faults?
Were any structural welds or joints visibly cracked?
Is the damage affecting the structural safety cage of the vehicle?
Was the inside of any door panel damaged (internal mechanism)?
Was the vehicle's front crossmember or subframe damaged?
Was the engine mounts displaced or cracked?
Is water visible inside the vehicle's cabin?
Was the vehicle's tow bar or towing hitch damaged?
Was the vehicle's roof rack or cargo carrier damaged?
Was the vehicle's parking camera or ultrasonic sensor cracked?
Were the vehicle's mud flaps damaged or torn off?
Was the vehicle's step board or running board bent or broken?
Was any other vehicle or person involved in the incident?
Was the other vehicle's registration number captured (photo or notes)?
What is the make and model of the other vehicle involved?
What was the colour of the other vehicle?
Was the other vehicle a two-wheeler, car, truck, or bus?
What is the name of the other driver involved?
Do you have the contact number of the other driver?
Do you know which insurance company covers the other vehicle?
What is the name of the other vehicle's insurance company?
What is the policy number of the other vehicle's insurance?
Was the other driver holding a valid driving licence?
Did you note down the other driver's driving licence number?
Was the other driver the owner of the vehicle?
Did the other driver appear to be under the influence of alcohol or drugs?
Was the other driver cooperative and willing to exchange details?
Did the other driver deny responsibility at the scene?
Was the other vehicle's registration document (RC book) verified at the scene?
Was the other vehicle carrying passengers?
Were any passengers in the other vehicle injured?
Was a pedestrian injured in the incident?
Did you take photos of the other vehicle's damage?
Was the other vehicle also damaged? Can you describe the extent?
Was the other vehicle towed away from the scene?
Was it a hit-and-run — did the other vehicle flee without stopping?
Did you manage to note the registration plate of the fleeing vehicle?
Was the fleeing vehicle's colour or type noted?
Did any bystanders witness the hit-and-run and note the vehicle details?
Did you file a police report specifically mentioning the hit-and-run?
Was the other driver aggressive or threatening at the scene?
Did the other driver or any bystander call the police?
Was the other vehicle a government or official vehicle?
Was the other vehicle an emergency vehicle (ambulance, fire truck, police car)?
Did the other driver receive any medical attention at the scene?
Was an FIR filed against the other driver?
Did the other driver attempt to settle privately without involving insurance?
Was a private settlement amount agreed upon at the scene?
Was the third party's vehicle stationary (parked) when the incident occurred?
Did the other driver have a dashcam recording of the incident?
Was the other vehicle heavily loaded or overloaded at the time?
Did the other driver leave contact details before leaving the scene?
Was the other vehicle displaying valid insurance papers at the scene?
Was the other vehicle registered in the same state as yours?
Was a third-party claim intimation already filed by you?
Did you receive any demand notice or legal notice from the third party?
Was the other driver a minor (below 18 years)?
Was the other vehicle a rented or hire car?
Was the pedestrian injured crossing a designated crosswalk?
Was the pedestrian hospitalised?
Was a police case filed for the pedestrian injury?
Did the third party's vehicle have valid PUC and fitness certificates?
Was the third party a foreign national or diplomatic vehicle?
Did the third party's insurer survey their vehicle independently?
Was the third party's vehicle repaired, and do you know the cost?
Was the third party a commercial vehicle operator (goods carrier)?
Did you receive any compensation demand from the third party's passengers?
Was the third party's vehicle insured under a fleet policy?
Did the third party agree to mediation or out-of-court settlement?
Was the third party vehicle ownership verified (not stolen)?
Did the third party's vehicle have any defects contributing to the incident?
Was the third party a repeat offender with prior accident history?
Was the third party under a driving ban or suspended licence?
Did the third party submit their vehicle for inspection?
Was the third party's address noted or verified at the scene?
Did both parties sign any form or agreement at the scene?
Was a mediator or traffic warden present to document the exchange?
Did any bystander photograph or video the incident for evidence?
Was the third party's vehicle number plate obscured or covered?
Did the third party deny being involved in the incident?
Was the third party's phone number verified and working?
Did the third party demand payment in cash to settle on the spot?
Was the third party's vehicle identified via CCTV footage later?
Was the third party an Ola/Uber or other app-based cab?
Did the third party's insurance company reach out to you directly?
Was the third party operating a vehicle with a learner's licence?
Was the third party's vehicle involved in any pending criminal cases?
Did the third party provide false contact details at the scene?
Did the police take statements from both parties at the scene?
Was the third party driving a vehicle registered in another country?
Was the third party carrying construction materials or sharp items that could have caused damage?
Did you exchange your policy details with the third party?
Was the third party's licence a commercial (heavy vehicle) licence?
Did the third party vehicle have a GPS tracker?
Was there a language barrier with the third party driver at the scene?
Was the third party operating a school bus or transport vehicle for children?
Did the third party suffer head injuries or whiplash?
Was the third party taken to a specific hospital after the incident?
Has any civil suit or court notice been served by the third party?
Was the third party a minor being driven by a parent or guardian?
Was the incident a chain reaction involving 3 or more vehicles?
Was the third party vehicle a hire/taxi vehicle with passengers at the time?
Did the third party vehicle carry any signage (delivery, ambulance, etc.)?
Was the other driver using a mobile phone at the time of the incident?
Was the third party's vehicle impounded by police after the incident?
Was a joint inspection of both vehicles conducted by a surveyor?
Did the third party's insurer deny the claim?
Was the third party an uninsured driver?
Was the third party driving a vehicle that was not registered in their name?
Was compensation for the third party's vehicle settled through an insurance tribunal?
Did the third party vehicle belong to a government department?
Was the third party's vehicle recently inspected for fitness?
Was a police report or FIR filed for this incident?
What is the FIR number?
Which police station was the FIR filed at?
When was the FIR filed?
Do you have a copy of the FIR?
Why was no police report filed?
Was the incident handled privately between both parties without a police report?
Were any witnesses present at the scene of the incident?
Can you provide the name and contact of a witness?
Was the witness an independent bystander or a person known to you?
Has the witness provided a written or recorded statement?
Is CCTV footage from the incident location available?
Has the CCTV footage been preserved or downloaded?
Do you have dashcam footage of the incident?
Is the dashcam footage timestamped and unedited?
Was a Motor Accident Claims Tribunal (MACT) petition filed?
Was any legal notice received from an injured party or third party?
Has a lawyer been engaged to handle the case?
Has the insurer been informed about the legal notice?
Was a charge sheet or challan issued by the police?
What was the reason for the challan (drunk driving, signal jump, overspeeding)?
Has the challan been paid?
Was the vehicle seized by the police?
Was the driving licence seized by the police?
Was a case filed under IPC or Motor Vehicles Act sections?
Was the driver arrested at the scene?
Was bail obtained after arrest?
Has a court date been set for the case?
Has the insurance company been informed about any court proceedings?
Was the incident reported to the RTO (Regional Transport Office)?
Was a spot panchanama (scene inspection report) prepared by the police?
Is a copy of the spot panchanama available?
Was the driver's licence number recorded in the FIR?
Was the other vehicle's registration noted in the FIR?
Was a breathalyser test administered by the police?
What was the breathalyser test result?
Was a blood sample collected for alcohol testing?
Was the incident declared a 'hit and run' in the FIR?
Was the incident declared a 'fatal accident' in the FIR?
Was a medicolegal case (MLC) opened at the hospital?
Do you have the MLC number?
Was the vehicle involved in a previous police case?
Was there any dispute about the sequence of events in the police statement?
Was a complaint filed against the other driver for negligent driving?
Has the police investigation been closed or is it ongoing?
Was a recovery notice sent to the third party by the insurer?
Was a consent letter given to the insurer to pursue recovery from the third party?
Was a subrogation letter signed?
Was a no-claim certificate or declaration provided to the police?
Was the police report consistent with your account of the incident?
Was an independent investigator appointed?
Did the police take photographs at the scene?
Was a sketch or diagram of the incident prepared by the police?
Was the vehicle towed under a police order?
Was the incident reported as a hit-and-run to the District Hit and Run Scheme?
Was the driver's statement consistent across police records and insurance records?
Was any ex-parte order passed against you in court?
Was the court judgment in your favour or against you?
Did the court direct the insurer to pay compensation?
Was the legal notice responded to within the required period?
Has any stay order been obtained on legal proceedings?
Did you file a police complaint about vandalism separately from the accident FIR?
Was the case registered under Section 279 (rash driving)?
Was the case registered under Section 304A (death due to negligence)?
Was a settlement reached at the Motor Accident Claims Tribunal level?
Was the police report filed within 24 hours of the incident?
Was any delay in filing the FIR explained to the police?
Was a written complaint to the police required by your insurer?
Was the alleged fault contested by the other driver in police records?
Was the incident captured in the police station's general diary (GD)?
Do you have the GD (general diary) entry number?
Has any criminal court issued summons to you related to the incident?
Was an insurance investigator appointed by the insurer for the claim?
Was the police case compounded (settled amicably with police approval)?
Has the driving licence been returned by the police after seizure?
Was a mutual divorce or guardianship order relevant in the case (commercial vehicle)?
Was a Road Transport Authority (RTA) inquiry initiated?
Was the vehicle confiscated as evidence?
Was a search warrant executed on the vehicle or premises?
Has the vehicle been released from police custody?
Was the incident related to an attempt to evade a police checkpoint?
Has the insurance company filed an intervention petition in the court case?
Did the police issue a No Objection Certificate (NOC) for the vehicle's release?
Was any advocate or legal representative present during the FIR filing?
Was the incident registered as an accident under the Motor Vehicles Act Section 134?
Was the other party's legal representative or insurer present at any proceedings?
Was the driving licence valid for the category of vehicle driven?
Does your driving licence carry any endorsement (prior offence)?
Did the police note the endorsement in their records?
Was any video evidence submitted to the police?
Was a final investigation report filed by the police?
Was the police closure report (B report or C report) obtained?
Was a traffic collision investigation team deployed to the scene?
Was any compensation paid under the Solatium Fund for hit-and-run?
Has the police filed a charge sheet (challan) in court?
Was a remand application filed in the court for the driver's custody?
Was the case referred to a Lok Adalat for settlement?
Was a public prosecutor assigned to the case?
Was a compounding application filed to settle the traffic violation?
Was a stay order obtained from the High Court on the lower court's direction?
Is your insurance policy currently active and valid?
What is your policy number?
What type of coverage does your policy include — comprehensive, third-party only, or own damage?
When does your policy expire?
With which insurance company is your vehicle insured?
Is the policy a standard policy or a long-term (multi-year) policy?
Was the policy premium paid on time for the current year?
Was there any lapse in the policy before renewal?
Was the policy renewed within the grace period?
Who is the named insured on the policy?
Is the claimant the same person as the policyholder?
What is the Insured Declared Value (IDV) of the vehicle on the policy?
Is the IDV up to date with the current market value of the vehicle?
Does the policy include a zero-depreciation (zero-dep) add-on?
Does the policy include roadside assistance coverage?
Does the policy include engine protection coverage?
Does the policy include consumables coverage?
Does the policy include Return to Invoice (RTI) coverage?
Does the policy include NCB (No Claim Bonus) protection?
Does the policy include key replacement coverage?
Does the policy include tyre protection coverage?
Does the policy include personal accident cover for the owner-driver?
Does the policy include passenger personal accident cover?
What is the current NCB (No Claim Bonus) percentage on your policy?
Have you filed a claim in the last 3 years that would affect the NCB?
How many claims have been filed on this policy in the past?
Was the policy a new policy, or was it a renewal?
If renewal, was the previous policy with the same insurer?
Was the NCB carried forward correctly from the previous policy?
Was the previous insurer's NCB certificate obtained?
Is the vehicle's registration certificate (RC book) in the same name as the policy?
Was the vehicle transferred from another owner before this policy?
Was the insurance policy transferred along with vehicle ownership?
Was the vehicle used for commercial purposes despite a personal-use policy?
Was the vehicle used for hire or reward at the time of the incident?
Was the driver listed as an authorised driver on the policy?
If the driver is not the owner, were they added as a named driver on the policy?
Was the driver's licence verified at the time of policy issuance?
Was the policy subject to any endorsements or exclusions specific to this vehicle?
Was the vehicle's current usage consistent with the policy's declared usage?
Was the insurer notified within the required timeframe after the incident?
What is the required notification period under your policy?
Was there any exclusion clause in the policy relevant to this incident type?
Does your policy cover damage caused by natural disasters (flood, cyclone)?
Does your policy cover theft of the vehicle?
Is the driver's age within the eligible range specified in the policy?
Is there a deductible (excess) applicable on this claim? If so, how much?
Was the policy a group policy or an individual policy?
Was the policy issued through an agent, broker, or directly?
Was the vehicle inspected at the time of policy issuance (break-in inspection)?
Was any previous damage declared at the time of policy issuance?
Is the policy under any dispute or litigation with the insurer?
Was the policy a co-insurance arrangement (multiple insurers)?
Does your insurer have a cashless network garage near the incident location?
Was the claim intimated via the insurer's helpline, app, or through an agent?
Was a claim reference number issued by the insurer?
Was a surveyor appointed by the insurer within the required period?
Has the surveyor completed their inspection?
Was the surveyor's report shared with you?
Did the surveyor assess the loss as partial or total?
Was there any disagreement with the surveyor's assessment?
Was an appointed independent surveyor requested by you?
Is the vehicle financed (under a loan) and does the financier need to be notified?
Was the financier's name noted on the policy as a loss payee?
Is a Hypothecation Termination letter available if the loan is paid off?

Was the vehicle part of a delivery service caught in flash floods during a route?
Was the vehicle in an underpass that flooded rapidly?
Was the vehicle trapped and the occupant rescued by emergency services?
Was the flood damage accompanied by hailstorm damage?
Was the vehicle insured for all weather events under the policy?
Was the vehicle dragged by flood current causing additional scratch and impact damage?
What type of vandalism occurred — keying, tyre slashing, window smash, graffiti, or multiple?
When was the vandalism discovered?
Where was the vehicle parked when vandalism occurred?
Was the vehicle parked in a public road, residential area, or private premises?
Was the vandalism discovered in the morning after overnight parking?
Were any suspects seen in the vicinity of the vehicle before vandalism?
Were the suspects identified by any witness?
Was the vandalism a targeted act or random?
Was there a personal dispute that could have motivated the vandalism?
Was a police complaint (NCR or FIR) filed for the vandalism?
Were there CCTV cameras near the parking location?
Was the CCTV footage reviewed, and did it capture the vandalism act?
Was the vandalism on the paint only, or did it penetrate into the body panels?
Were all four tyres slashed, or only specific ones?
Was the windshield smashed or cracked?
Were any side windows broken?
Was any graphic, symbol, or slogan sprayed or written on the vehicle?
Was the graffiti content identifiable as a gang marking or political symbol?
Was the vehicle keyed (scratched with a sharp object)?
Was the keying across multiple panels or localised to one area?
Were door handles, mirrors, or antennas broken off?
Was the vehicle's registration plate removed or damaged?
Were any items stolen from the vehicle during the vandalism?
Was the vehicle's interior also vandalised (seats slashed, dashboard broken)?
Were the headlamps or tail lamps smashed?
Was the vehicle's bonnet opened and engine components damaged?
Was the vehicle set on fire after the vandalism?
Were other vehicles in the same area also vandalised (indicating a random spree)?
Was the area known for vehicle vandalism incidents?
Was the vandalism act during a public event, protest, or riot?
Was the vehicle of a public figure or official and therefore targeted?
Were there scuff marks, tool marks, or physical evidence of the vandal's tools?
Was any threatening note or message left on the vehicle?
Was the vandalism witnessed by a neighbour or bystander?
Did any witness see the vandal's face or description?
Was the vehicle parked in a gated society, and was there a guard?
Was the security guard's statement obtained?
Was the vandalism on a hired or rented vehicle?
Was the rental company informed about the vandalism?
Was the damage repairable with paint correction, or does it require panel replacement?
Was the vehicle a high-end brand making it a target for envy-based vandalism?
Was a parking dispute or altercation a possible trigger for the vandalism?
Was the owner threatened verbally before the vandalism occurred?
Was the vandalism committed during daytime or nighttime?
Was the vandalism on the vehicle's front, back, left, or right side?
Was the vehicle parked at the same location for several days before vandalism?
Was the vandalism in an area covered by the police beat patrol?
Was the vandalism case registered as a cognisable offence?
Were any fingerprints collected from the damaged vehicle by the police?
Was paint transfer from the vandalising tool found on the vehicle?
Was the vehicle covered under comprehensive insurance including vandalism?
Was the damage purely cosmetic, or did it affect the vehicle's structural integrity?
Were the wipers or wiper blades snapped off?
Was the radio antenna snapped off?
Was a private investigator hired to identify the vandal?
Was the vandalism act consistent with a known pattern of similar crimes in the area?
Was the police investigation status active, closed, or chargesheeted?
Was any compensation received from a civil suit against the vandal?
Was the vandalism act reported on social media by the perpetrator?
Was any mobile phone or smart device evidence used to identify the vandal?
Were the damage repair costs higher than the vehicle's market value?
Was the vehicle wrapped in vinyl, and was the wrap also damaged?
Was the vehicle's personalised (private) number plate stolen during vandalism?
Was a special paint protection film (PPF) on the vehicle also damaged?
Was the convertible hood or soft top of a convertible slashed?
Were there any communal or ethnic overtones to the vandalism?
Was the vandalism on a vehicle used in political campaigning?
Was the vandalism act video recorded by the perpetrator and shared?
Was the vehicle's company logo or decal targeted (commercial vehicle)?
Were door handles torn off in the vandalism?
Was the underbody of the vehicle damaged by a sharp object during vandalism?
Was the fuel cap tampered with or removed during vandalism?
Was any foreign substance (sugar, water) poured into the fuel tank?
Were the ignition or door lock cylinders damaged?
Was a bomb threat or explosive hoax involved in the vandalism report?
Was the windshield vandalism covered under the glass damage benefit?
Were any high-value accessories (alloy wheels, stereo) stolen during vandalism?
Were multiple scratches or dents concentrated in one area (indicating one attacker)?
Was the vandalism done during a community celebration or cricket match?
Was the vandalism a copycat of another well-known incident in the media?
Was a formal apology or compensation offered by the perpetrator directly?
Did the vehicle owner install any new security features in response to prior vandalism?
Was the vehicle's keyless entry system hacked or jammed during vandalism?
Was any part of the chassis or frame scratched to record/erase the chassis number?
Was the rear-view camera lens damaged?
Was the vehicle's spare tyre locked and was the lock broken in the vandalism?
Was any adhesive, tar, or chemical substance applied to the vehicle's exterior?
Was the vehicle inside a covered car parking when vandalism occurred?
Was the vandalism by a child or minor (unintentional) rather than deliberate?
Was the damage to the vehicle's tinted windows specifically targeted?
Was the claim for vandalism filed within the policy's notification window?
Was the surveyor's assessment consistent with the type of vandalism described?
Was the vandalised vehicle a new purchase with less than 6 months of ownership?
Was the vehicle's catalytic converter stolen as part of the vandalism?
Was the damage limited to a single deep scratch across multiple panels?
Was the vandalism in retaliation for a traffic confrontation earlier that day?
Was the vehicle's charging port (for EVs) damaged or blocked?
Was the vandalism claim supported by a local news report of widespread vandalism?
Was the vehicle's dashboard GPS device stolen during the vandalism?
Was the vehicle security system disabled remotely before the vandalism?
